title: David and Goliath
subtitle: The Boy Who Walked Into the Shadow
author: YourWord

style_notes.description: Words styled with size, weight, color, and italics to create visual drama. Use Flutter RichText or HTML spans to render.

pages.heading: The Valley of Elah
pages.body: The Philistine army lined the ridge like a *wall of iron*. Forty thousand sandals pressed into the dust. The air smelled of **SWEAT**, leather, and something older — like the earth before a storm. Israel's soldiers stood on the opposite hill, not moving. Just... watching.

pages.heading: The Giant Speaks
pages.body: Then he came out. **GOLIATH.** Nine feet tall. Bronze helmet. Spear like a *weaver's beam*. His voice didn't shout — it *rolled* across the valley like THUNDER through a cave. 'Send me a man,' he said. His knuckles were scarred. He had done this before.

pages.heading: The Boy With Bread
pages.body: David arrived carrying *cheese*. His father sent him to check on his brothers — not to fight, just to deliver lunch. He was the youngest. The one left behind with the sheep. He heard Goliath's voice and his *jaw tightened*. He asked the same question twice, to two different men.

pages.heading: His Brothers Were Angry
pages.body: Eliab — David's oldest brother — *grabbed his arm*. 'Why are you even here? Who's watching those few sheep?' His voice was sharp. His eyes were sharper. David turned away from him **calmly**. That was the contradiction: the younger one wasn't afraid of the giant. He was used to being dismissed by the people who should've believed in him.

pages.heading: Before the King
pages.body: King Saul looked at David the way adults look at children who've said something *inconvenient*. 'You're just a boy,' he said. David's tunic was dusty. His hands smelled like grass. He said: **'I killed a lion. I killed a bear. This Philistine will be like one of them.'** Saul had no answer for that.

pages.heading: The Armor That Didn't Fit
pages.body: Saul tried to dress him in *bronze armor*. A helmet. A coat of mail. David put it on, took two steps, and stopped. He took it all off. He walked to the stream instead. He reached in with his hand and pulled out **five smooth stones**. Cold. Round. *Perfect.*

pages.heading: The Walk Across the Valley
pages.body: Goliath saw him coming and *laughed*. An actual laugh. He looked at the shepherd boy and said, 'Am I a dog, that you come at me with sticks?' The ground between them was **DRY**, cracked, silent. David's feet were bare. He could feel every pebble. He kept walking.

pages.heading: One Stone
pages.body: David reached into his bag. His fingers closed around the first stone — cold, slightly *damp* from the stream. He swung the sling once. Twice. The stone left his hand at a speed no eye could follow. It struck Goliath in the *center of his forehead*. The ground shook when he fell. **The whole valley went quiet.**

style_hints.The whole valley went quiet: large, then fade to small at end
